For more details please see https://wiki.hpdd.intel.com/display/PUB/Coding+Guidelines

Patch Set 8: Code-Review-1

(53 comments)

I'm not sure why all of the mapping functions changed from returning the qtype or similar, and now take the return value as an argument and return void?  That makes it difficult to stack these functions or return an error for unknown quota types.

It seems there are still too many qtype2*() and *2qtype() mapping functions, and it seems at least some of them could be removed.

The usage of MAXQUOTAS in the Lustre code should all be replaced with LL_MAXQUOTAS so that it is independent of what the kernel defines MAXQUOTAS to be, since I don't think the Lustre code interacts directly with the kernel quota code at all.

Patch Set 8:

(15 comments)

For more details please see http://wiki.lustre.org/Lustre_Coding_Style_Guidelines

Patch Set 8: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

parents.subject: LU-4017 ldiskfs: add project quota support

commit.subject: LU-4017 quota: cleanup codes of quota for new type
commit.message:
LU-4017 quota: cleanup codes of quota for new type

This patch cleanups codes of quota to prepare for
new type other than USRQUOTA/GRPQUOTA.

Signed-off-by: Li Xi <lixi@ddn.com>
Signed-off-by: Wang Shilong <wshilong@ddn.com>
Signed-off-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>

Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/18894
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Niu Yawei <yawei.niu@intel.com>
